wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the WheelSense Platform project overview. Explain the platform's purpose as an IoT + clinical workflow solution for wheelchair monitoring, room localization, patient workflows, smart-device control, and role-based web dashboards. Document the system architecture, core technologies (FastAPI, Next.js, Arduino firmware), and key value propositions. Include the project's mission, target audience (healthcare facilities), and competitive advantages. Provide high-level system diagrams showing the relationship between backend, frontend, firmware, and AI components. Address the platform's scalability, security considerations, and integration capabilities. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common use cases.

Provide practical examples of application initialization, error handling patterns, and configuration best practices.

<blog>

# WheelSense Platform: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

WheelSense is an innovative IoT + clinical workflow platform designed for wheelchair monitoring, room localization, patient workflows, smart-device control, and role-based web dashboards. The platform serves healthcare facilities by providing real-time patient tracking, automated alerts, and intelligent workflow management through a sophisticated three-layer architecture.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Real-time Patient Monitoring**: Track wheelchair movement and patient location using advanced RSSI-based localization
- **Intelligent Workflow Automation**: Streamline clinical workflows through AI-powered agent runtime and MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration
- **Role-Based Access Control**: Provide secure, role-specific dashboards for administrators, head nurses, supervisors, observers, and patients
- **IoT Device Integration**: Support multiple device types including wheelchair sensors, camera nodes, and smart home devices
- **Clinical Decision Support**: Offer AI-assisted decision making through secure, scope-limited tool execution

### Target Audience

- Healthcare facilities seeking automated patient monitoring solutions
- Clinical staff requiring real-time workflow management tools
- Administrators managing device fleets and facility operations
- Patients needing accessible healthcare portal interfaces

## Technical Architecture

The WheelSense platform employs a distributed microservices architecture with three primary runtime layers, each serving distinct functional domains:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Layer] --> B[Web Application]
    B --> C[API Gateway]
    
    C --> D[FastAPI Backend]
    D --> E[PostgreSQL Database]
    D --> F[MQTT Broker]
    
    D --> G[Agent Runtime]
    G --> H[MCP Server]
    H --> I[AI Tools]
    
    J[Firmware Layer] --> K[Wheelchair Device]
    J --> L[Camera Node]
    K --> M[Telemetry Collection]
    L --> N[Camera Control]
    
    M --> O[MQTT Transport]
    N --> O
    O --> D
    
    P[Frontend Layer] --> B
    Q[Mobile Apps] --> B
```

### Component Breakdown

**Layer 1: Firmware Layer**
- **M5StickCPlus2**: Wheelchair device firmware collecting IMU, motion, RSSI, and battery telemetry
- **Node_Tsimcam**: Camera and BLE beacon node for room identification and image capture
- **Real-time Data Collection**: Continuous sensor data streaming via MQTT protocol

**Layer 2: Backend Services**
- **FastAPI Application**: Primary API gateway with comprehensive healthcare domain coverage
- **PostgreSQL Database**: Persistent storage for patient records, device configurations, and workflow data
- **MQTT Broker**: Real-time message bus for device telemetry and control commands
- **Agent Runtime**: AI-powered workflow orchestrator with secure tool execution

**Layer 3: Frontend Applications**
- **Next.js Web App**: Role-based dashboards for administrative and clinical workflows
- **Mobile Interfaces**: Patient portal and staff mobile applications
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Live floorplan visualization and alert systems

### Design Patterns

The platform implements several key architectural patterns:

- **Microservices Pattern**: Separate services for different healthcare domains (patients, devices, workflow, analytics)
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: MQTT-based pub/sub for real-time device communication
- **CQRS Pattern**: Separate read and write models for optimal performance
- **Domain-Driven Design**: Clear separation of healthcare-specific business domains
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access layer for database operations

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**
- `server/app/main.py`: FastAPI application initialization with lifespan management
- Handles database initialization, MQTT listener startup, and retention scheduler configuration

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `frontend/next.config.ts`: Next.js configuration with API proxy settings
- Manages redirects and standalone output for containerized deployment

**Firmware Entry Point**
- `firmware/M5StickCPlus2/src/main.cpp`: Arduino-based device initialization
- Manages sensor collection, network connectivity, and MQTT publishing

### Core Modules

**MQTT Integration**
- `server/app/mqtt_handler.py`: Centralized MQTT message processing and device registration
- Handles wheelchair telemetry, camera status, and device control commands

**AI Agent Runtime**
- `server/app/agent_runtime/`: Secure AI orchestration with intent classification and tool execution
- Supports both regex-based intent routing and LLM-native tool calling

**Authentication & Authorization**
- `server/app/api/dependencies.py`: Role-based access control with workspace scoping
- Implements JWT-based session management with admin impersonation capabilities

**Device Management**
- `server/app/services/device_management.py`: Registry management for wheelchairs and camera nodes
- Handles device lifecycle, patient assignment, and command routing

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `server/app/config.py`: Runtime settings with Docker Compose integration
- Supports simulator mode, production mode, and development configurations

**Frontend Configuration**
- `frontend/components/providers/AppProviders.tsx`: Root provider setup with theme, auth, and state management
- `frontend/lib/api.ts`: API client with automatic authentication and error handling

**Database Migrations**
- `server/alembic/versions/`: Structured schema evolution with version control
- Maintains backward compatibility during system upgrades

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- FastAPI 0.115.0: Asynchronous web framework with automatic OpenAPI generation
- SQLAlchemy 2.0: ORM for PostgreSQL database operations
- MQTT Client: Real-time message processing and device communication
- Alembic: Database schema migration management

**Frontend Dependencies**
- Next.js 16.2.2: App router with server-side rendering and static generation
- React 19.2.4: Component-based UI framework with concurrent features
- TanStack Query 5.96.2: Server state management with caching and synchronization
- Zustand 5.0.12: Lightweight state management for authentication and UI state

**Firmware Dependencies**
- Arduino Framework: Cross-platform embedded development
- M5StickCPlus2 Library: Hardware abstraction for device-specific features
- ArduinoJson: Efficient JSON serialization for MQTT payloads

### Integration Points

**MCP (Model Context Protocol) Integration**
- `server/app/mcp_server.py`: Secure AI tool execution with scope-based authorization
- Supports 28 workspace tools across 8 healthcare domains
- Provides OAuth 2.0 integration with scope narrowing capabilities

**Home Assistant Integration**
- `server/homeassistant/`: Smart home device control and automation
- Bridge between WheelSense devices and Home Assistant ecosystem
- Supports room automation and device control workflows

**Camera Integration**
- `firmware/Node_Tsimcam/`: Dedicated camera node with BLE beacon functionality
- Image capture and transmission via MQTT protocol
- Registration and status reporting for device lifecycle management

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant MQTT
    participant Device
    
    Client->>Frontend: User Interaction
    Frontend->>Backend: API Request
    Backend->>Backend: Authentication & Authorization
    Backend->>MQTT: Process Command
    MQTT->>Device: Send Command
    Device->>MQTT: Telemetry Response
    MQTT->>Backend: Receive Data
    Backend->>Backend: Process & Store
    Backend->>Frontend: Response
    Frontend->>Client: Update UI
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Patient Monitoring**
- Advanced RSSI-based room localization with machine learning predictions
- IMU sensor fusion for activity recognition and fall detection
- Continuous telemetry streaming with automatic device registration

**Secure AI Integration**
- Intent-based classification for natural language processing
- LLM-native tool routing with fallback mechanisms
- 3-stage chat action flow (propose → confirm → execute) with risk assessment

**Role-Based Workflows**
- Administrative dashboards with comprehensive oversight capabilities
- Clinical staff workflows with patient assignment and task management
- Patient portal with self-service capabilities and care coordination

### Implementation Highlights

**Device Fleet Management**
- `server/app/services/device_management.py`: Centralized device registry and lifecycle management
- Automatic device discovery and registration with workspace scoping
- Patient-device assignment with conflict resolution and audit trails

**Workflow Orchestration**
- `server/app/services/workflow.py`: Task and schedule management across healthcare domains
- Multi-step workflow execution with progress tracking and completion notifications
- Integration with clinical protocols and care pathways

**Analytics & Reporting**
- `server/app/services/analytics.py`: Real-time metrics and historical trend analysis
- Customizable reporting with role-based access to sensitive health information
- Integration with electronic health record systems

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> DeviceRegistration
    DeviceRegistration --> DeviceReady: Successful Registration
    DeviceRegistration --> DeviceError: Registration Failed
    
    DeviceReady --> TelemetryCollection: Active Device
    DeviceReady --> DeviceDisabled: Inactive Device
    
    TelemetryCollection --> RSSIPrediction: RSSI Available
    TelemetryCollection --> MotionAnalysis: IMU Data Available
    
    RSSIPrediction --> RoomAssignment: Prediction Confirmed
    MotionAnalysis --> ActivityRecognition: Motion Detected
    
    RoomAssignment --> AlertTrigger: Patient Movement
    ActivityRecognition --> FallDetection: Abnormal Activity
    
    AlertTrigger --> NotificationDelivery: Alert Created
    FallDetection --> EmergencyResponse: Critical Event
    
    NotificationDelivery --> [*]
    EmergencyResponse --> [*]
    DeviceError --> [*]
    DeviceDisabled --> [*]
```

**Smart Device Control**
- Room automation through Home Assistant integration
- Camera control and image capture for patient monitoring
- Device command queuing with acknowledgment and error handling

**Shift Checklists**
- Personalized daily checklists with completion tracking
- Template-based customization for different staff roles
- Integration with workflow management and care protocols

**Communication Systems**
- Role-based messaging with attachment support
- Workflow message threading with audit trails
- Integration with healthcare communication standards

---
